perturbation.original_text: Definition of Confidential Information; Use and Disclosure . “ Confidential Information ” means any information, technical data or know-how, including, but not limited to, that which relates to research, product or service plans, business practices, agreement terms, products, services, employees, suppliers, customers, technology or other strategic partners, stockholders, markets, software, know-how, developments, inventions, processes, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, finances, notes, analyses or studies and all tangible and intangible embodiments thereof of any kind whatsoever, whether conveyed in writing or orally by the disclosing party or its Associates to the receiving party or its Associates in connection with the evaluation of a Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Definition of Confidential Information; Use and Disclosure . “ Confidential Information ” means any information, technical data or know-how, including, but not limited to, that which relates to research, product or service plans, business practices, agreement terms, products, services, employees, suppliers, customers, technology or other strategic partners, stockholders, markets, software, know-how, developments, inventions, processes, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, finances, notes, analyses or studies and all tangible and intangible embodiments thereof of any kind whatsoever, whether conveyed in writing or orally by the disclosing party or its Associates to the receiving party or its Associates in connection with the evaluation of a Transaction. **However, for the purposes of Section 9 (Commercial Relationship Use), Confidential Information shall exclude information relating to flaws or problems with Company’s products, software, services or intellectual property rights.**
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an exception to the definition of 'Confidential Information' specifically for Section 9. While section 1 broadly defines Confidential Information, the added text excludes information about flaws in the company's products, software, etc., when used in the context of commercial relationships as described in Section 9. This directly contradicts the broad definition in Section 1, as such flaws would normally fall under 'technical data or know-how' relating to products or services. This creates an In-Text contradiction in Section 9, where it is unclear if information about flaws should be treated as Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Commercial Relationship Use . Notwithstanding any obligations or restrictions to the contrary contained herein, if during due diligence review, HPE learns of flaws or problems with Company’s products, software, services or intellectual property rights, the Associates of HPE evaluating the potential Transaction may share such information with the Associates of HPE who are responsible for purchasing such products, software or services or licensing such intellectual property on behalf of HPE (“Purchasing Associates”). For avoidance of doubt, Purchasing Associates of the receiving party that receive such information agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. In the event any Confidential Information is disclosed pursuant to this Section 9, HPE shall notify the Company of any information shared with Associates of HPE within fifteen (15) business days after such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the definition of 'Confidential Information' for Section 9, excluding information about flaws in the company's products. The contradicted text in Section 9 discusses the sharing of information about flaws with HPE associates, implicitly treating such information as Confidential Information by stating 'In the event any Confidential Information is disclosed pursuant to this Section 9'. The added exception in the definition creates a direct contradiction because Section 9 now references the definition of Confidential Information while simultaneously describing information that is explicitly excluded from that definition for the purposes of Section 9.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party shall inform its Associates of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and shall instruct them to treat the information confidentially. The receiving party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Associates and agrees, at its sole expense, to take all commercially reasonable measures to restrain its Associates from disclosure or use of the Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party shall inform its Associates of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and shall instruct them to treat the information confidentially. The receiving party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Associates **except in cases of gross negligence, for which the Associate is solely responsible.** and agrees, at its sole expense, to take all commercially reasonable measures to restrain its Associates from disclosure or use of the Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an exception to the receiving party's responsibility for breaches by its Associates. The original text makes the receiving party fully responsible. This creates an In-Text contradiction with the remainder of the paragraph, where it specifies the receiving party's obligations to prevent breaches, implying full responsibility, but the inserted text removes that responsibility for gross negligence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition of Confidential Information; Use and Disclosure . “ Confidential Information ” means any information, technical data or know-how, including, but not limited to, that which relates to research, product or service plans, business practices, agreement terms, products, services, employees, suppliers, customers, technology or other strategic partners, stockholders, markets, software, know-how, developments, inventions, processes, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, finances, notes, analyses or studies and all tangible and intangible embodiments thereof of any kind whatsoever, whether conveyed in writing or orally by the disclosing party or its Associates to the receiving party or its Associates in connection with the evaluation of a Transaction. The term “Confidential Information” shall be deemed to include those portions of any notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations, memoranda or other documents (regardless of the form thereof) prepared by the receiving party or its Associates which contain, reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, any information furnished to the receiving party or its Associates pursuant hereto. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Confidential Information does not include information, technical data or know-how which: (i) is in the possession of the receiving party or its Associates at the time of disclosure, as shown by files and records immediately prior to the time of disclosure; (ii) prior to or after the time of disclosure becomes part of the public knowledge or literature, not as a result of any inaction or action of the receiving party or its Associates; (iii) is obtained by the receiving party or its Associates from a source other than the disclosing party or its Associates, which source would not be reasonably expected by the receiving party or its Associates to have any obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing party with respect to such information; (iv) is approved, in writing, for release by the disclosing party; or (v) the receiving party or its Associates can document was independently developed by the receiving party or its Associates without use of or reference to the disclosing party’s Confidential Information. Each of the parties hereto agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party or its Associates except in connection with preparing a proposal for and any discussions of a Transaction or as otherwise permitted hereunder. Neither party will disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to third parties except those of its Associates who are required to have the information in order to evaluate and discuss a Transaction. The receiving party shall inform its Associates of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and shall instruct them to treat the information confidentially. The receiving party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Associates except in cases of gross negligence, for which the Associate is solely responsible. and agrees, at its sole expense, to take all commercially reasonable measures to restrain its Associates from disclosure or use of the Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement. Each party agrees that it will take commercially reasonable measures to protect the confidentiality of, and to avoid having Confidential Information of the other party enter the public domain, become publicly available, or be held by persons not authorized hereunder to have such Confidential Information, which measures shall include at least the same degree of care that the receiving party utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Each party agrees to notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information of the other party that may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the receiving party's responsibility for breaches by its Associates, specifically in cases of gross negligence. The original text held the receiving party fully responsible for any breach by its Associates, but now the receiving party isn't responsible for gross negligence. The contradicted text includes the changed text, resulting in an in-text contradiction within the Confidential Information section itself. This undermines the consistent application of confidentiality obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The existence and the terms of this Agreement, any Transaction, the fact that any Confidential Information has been provided to the other party, and the existence, nature and status of any discussions between the parties shall be treated as Confidential Information hereunder, shall be maintained in strict confidence by the parties hereto and by their respective Associates and, subject to Section 3 of this Agreement, shall not be disclosed to any third party.
perturbation.changed_text: The existence and the terms of this Agreement, any Transaction, the fact that any Confidential Information has been provided to the other party, and the existence, nature and status of any discussions between the parties shall be treated as Confidential Information hereunder, shall be maintained in strict confidence by the parties hereto and by their respective Associates and, subject to Section 3 of this Agreement, shall not be disclosed to any third party **unless required to be disclosed to comply with local business laws or regulations.**
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits disclosure of the Agreement's existence or terms. The added clause allows disclosure if required by local business laws, creating an In-Text contradiction. Section 2 states these must be maintained in strict confidence but now the edited text allows it to be disclosed, making it ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nonpublicity . The existence and the terms of this Agreement, any Transaction, the fact that any Confidential Information has been provided to the other party, and the existence, nature and status of any discussions between the parties shall be treated as Confidential Information hereunder, shall be maintained in strict confidence by the parties hereto and by their respective Associates and, subject to Section 3 of this Agreement, shall not be disclosed to any third party unless required to be disclosed to comply with local business laws or regulations.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated strict confidentiality, prohibiting disclosure of the Agreement's existence or terms. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing disclosure if required by local business laws or regulations. This directly contradicts the original intent of strict confidentiality as defined in the contradicted text which now shares the same contradicting language. The explanation provided clearly defines that this change creates an In-Text contradiction based on the definition.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that it will take commercially reasonable measures to protect the confidentiality of, and to avoid having Confidential Information of the other party enter the public domain, become publicly available, or be held by persons not authorized hereunder to have such Confidential Information, which measures shall include at least the same degree of care that the receiving party utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that it will take commercially reasonable measures to protect the confidentiality of, and to avoid having Confidential Information of the other party enter the public domain, become publicly available, or be held by persons not authorized hereunder to have such Confidential Information, which measures shall include at least the same degree of care that the receiving party utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. **However, if a data breach occurs despite these measures, the receiving party will not be held liable unless the breach was a direct result of intentional misconduct.**
perturbation.explanation: The original text implies strict liability for failing to protect Confidential Information. The added text limits liability to cases of intentional misconduct, creating an In-Text contradiction. This limits liability and creates an ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition of Confidential Information; Use and Disclosure . “ Confidential Information ” means any information, technical data or know-how, including, but not limited to, that which relates to research, product or service plans, business practices, agreement terms, products, services, employees, suppliers, customers, technology or other strategic partners, stockholders, markets, software, know-how, developments, inventions, processes, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, finances, notes, analyses or studies and all tangible and intangible embodiments thereof of any kind whatsoever, whether conveyed in writing or orally by the disclosing party or its Associates to the receiving party or its Associates in connection with the evaluation of a Transaction. The term “Confidential Information” shall be deemed to include those portions of any notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations, memoranda or other documents (regardless of the form thereof) prepared by the receiving party or its Associates which contain, reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, any information furnished to the receiving party or its Associates pursuant hereto. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Confidential Information does not include information, technical data or know-how which: (i) is in the possession of the receiving party or its Associates at the time of disclosure, as shown by files and records immediately prior to the time of disclosure; (ii) prior to or after the time of disclosure becomes part of the public knowledge or literature, not as a result of any inaction or action of the receiving party or its Associates; (iii) is obtained by the receiving party or its Associates from a source other than the disclosing party or its Associates, which source would not be reasonably expected by the receiving party or its Associates to have any obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing party with respect to such information; (iv) is approved, in writing, for release by the disclosing party; or (v) the receiving party or its Associates can document was independently developed by the receiving party or its Associates without use of or reference to the disclosing party’s Confidential Information. Each of the parties hereto agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party or its Associates except in connection with preparing a proposal for and any discussions of a Transaction or as otherwise permitted hereunder. Neither party will disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to third parties except those of its Associates who are required to have the information in order to evaluate and discuss a Transaction. The receiving party shall inform its Associates of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and shall instruct them to treat the information confidentially. The receiving party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Associates except in cases of gross negligence, for which the Associate is solely responsible. and agrees, at its sole expense, to take all commercially reasonable measures to restrain its Associates from disclosure or use of the Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement. Each party agrees that it will take commercially reasonable measures to protect the confidentiality of, and to avoid having Confidential Information of the other party enter the public domain, become publicly available, or be held by persons not authorized hereunder to have such Confidential Information, which measures shall include at least the same degree of care that the receiving party utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. However, if a data breach occurs despite these measures, the receiving party will not be held liable unless the breach was a direct result of intentional misconduct. Each party agrees to notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information of the other party that may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The original text in the contradicted section states, 'Each party agrees that it will take commercially reasonable measures to protect the confidentiality of...Confidential Information, which measures shall include at least the same degree of care that the receiving party utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature.' This implies a certain level of responsibility for protecting confidential information. However, the changed text adds, 'However, if a data breach occurs despite these measures, the receiving party will not be held liable unless the breach was a direct result of intentional misconduct.' This limits the receiving party's liability, even if they failed to take commercially reasonable measures, creating a contradiction with the previous statement which implied a stronger responsibility. The Changed Text directly narrows liability described in the rest of the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: Following written notice by one party to another of the termination of discussions between the parties, upon written request of the disclosing party, the receiving party shall take reasonable steps to instruct all persons involved in the Transaction to destroy all Confidential Information furnished to the receiving party by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant to this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the receiving party and its Associates may retain any Confidential Information to the extent required pursuant to the regulatory compliance or record retention policies of such receiving party or Associate.
perturbation.changed_text: Following written notice by one party to another of the termination of discussions between the parties, upon written request of the disclosing party, the receiving party shall take reasonable steps to instruct all persons involved in the Transaction to destroy all Confidential Information furnished to the receiving party by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant to this Agreement. **However, the receiving party may also retain copies of Confidential Information for archival purposes and business continuity planning.** Notwithstanding the foregoing, the receiving party and its Associates may retain any Confidential Information to the extent required pursuant to the regulatory compliance or record retention policies of such receiving party or Associate.
perturbation.explanation: The added text introduces another reason for retaining Confidential Information beyond regulatory compliance: archival purposes and business continuity planning. This contradicts the initial instruction to destroy all Confidential Information and creates In-Text contradiction with the 'regulatory compliance' exception, as it adds a vague and potentially broad exception. This change makes it difficult to determine the scope of allowable retention.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination of Discussions . Following written notice by one party to another of the termination of discussions between the parties, upon written request of the disclosing party, the receiving party shall take reasonable steps to instruct all persons involved in the Transaction to destroy all Confidential Information furnished to the receiving party by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant to this Agreement. However, the receiving party may also retain copies of Confidential Information for archival purposes and business continuity planning. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the receiving party and its Associates may retain any Confidential Information to the extent required pursuant to the regulatory compliance or record retention policies of such receiving party or Associate.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'archival purposes and business continuity planning' as a reason to retain Confidential Information, in addition to regulatory compliance or record retention policies. This contradicts the initial instruction to destroy all Confidential Information and also creates an ambiguity/In-Text Contradiction, as it adds a vague and potentially broad exception. The Contradicted Text, which is in the same location, now includes this additional reason for retention, creating a direct conflict with the instruction to destroy all confidential information, which aligns with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The foregoing commitments of either party in this Agreement regarding the confidentiality and non-use of Confidential Information shall survive any termination of discussions between the parties and shall continue for a period of eighteen (18) months following the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The foregoing commitments of either party in this Agreement regarding the confidentiality and non-use of Confidential Information shall survive any termination of discussions between the parties and shall continue for a period of eighteen (18) months following the date of this Agreement. **However, for information relating to pricing and customer lists, these commitments shall only last for six (6) months.**
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces different durations for confidentiality depending on the type of Confidential Information. Section 11, which defines the term of confidentiality, has been modified. The new text states that the commitment to confidentiality lasts for 18 months, but information regarding pricing and customer lists only lasts for six months. This directly contracts the first statement, since Pricing and Customer lists fall under the definition of confidential information. This new definition impacts the rest of the agreement which relies on the single definition of the term in section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition of Confidential Information; Use and Disclosure . “ Confidential Information ” means any information, technical data or know-how, including, but not limited to, that which relates to research, product or service plans, business practices, agreement terms, products, services, employees, suppliers, customers, technology or other strategic partners, stockholders, markets, software, know-how, developments, inventions, processes, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, finances, notes, analyses or studies and all tangible and intangible embodiments thereof of any kind whatsoever, whether conveyed in writing or orally by the disclosing party or its Associates to the receiving party or its Associates in connection with the evaluation of a Transaction. The term “Confidential Information” shall be deemed to include those portions of any notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations, memoranda or other documents (regardless of the form thereof) prepared by the receiving party or its Associates which contain, reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, any information furnished to the receiving party or its Associates pursuant hereto. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Confidential Information does not include information, technical data or know-how which: (i) is in the possession of the receiving party or its Associates at the time of disclosure, as shown by files and records immediately prior to the time of disclosure; (ii) prior to or after the time of disclosure becomes part of the public knowledge or literature, not as a result of any inaction or action of the receiving party or its Associates; (iii) is obtained by the receiving party or its Associates from a source other than the disclosing party or its Associates, which source would not be reasonably expected by the receiving party or its Associates to have any obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing party with respect to such information; (iv) is approved, in writing, for release by the disclosing party; or (v) the receiving party or its Associates can document was independently developed by the receiving party or its Associates without use of or reference to the disclosing party’s Confidential Information. Each of the parties hereto agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party or its Associates except in connection with preparing a proposal for and any discussions of a Transaction or as otherwise permitted hereunder. Neither party will disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to third parties except those of its Associates who are required to have the information in order to evaluate and discuss a Transaction. The receiving party shall inform its Associates of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and shall instruct them to treat the information confidentially. The receiving party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Associates except in cases of gross negligence, for which the Associate is solely responsible. and agrees, at its sole expense, to take all commercially reasonable measures to restrain its Associates from disclosure or use of the Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement. Each party agrees that it will take commercially reasonable measures to protect the confidentiality of, and to avoid having Confidential Information of the other party enter the public domain, become publicly available, or be held by persons not authorized hereunder to have such Confidential Information, which measures shall include at least the same degree of care that the receiving party utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. However, if a data breach occurs despite these measures, the receiving party will not be held liable unless the breach was a direct result of intentional misconduct. Each party agrees to notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information of the other party that may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the duration of the confidentiality commitment for specific types of information (pricing and customer lists) to six months, while the original text stipulated an 18-month period for all confidential information. The 'Contradicted Text' defines 'Confidential Information' broadly, including customer information, without specifying different durations. Therefore, the changed text creates a direct contradiction by introducing a shorter confidentiality period for information that falls under the initial definition of 'Confidential Information' in another section of the agreement, which is an in-text contradiction.
